denial_letter_references.title: Internal claims and appeals — diagnosis/treatment codes (29 CFR § 2590.715-2719)
denial_letter_references.relevance: Calibrated P2 letter surface: Claim identification (DOS, provider, amount).

denial_letter_text:
Dear Member,

Cigna has completed its review of the prior authorization request your provider submitted on May 22, 2026 for renal denervation (catheter-based renal sympathetic denervation) to treat resistant (refractory) hypertension (I10). After review, this request is not approved at this time.

The requested procedure is to be performed by an out-of-network provider. Under the terms of your plan, out-of-network services are not covered without an approved network gap exception, and our records do not reflect that such an exception has been granted. In-network options for managing your blood pressure are available, and on that basis the out-of-network request is not authorized.

Under our current Cigna Medical Coverage Policy, renal denervation is moreover considered experimental or investigational for this indication. We also applied our geriatric hypertension management criteria, under which the requested procedural intervention is not supported. For these reasons the request does not meet our criteria for approval.

You or your provider may request, at no charge, a copy of the claim file and the specific clinical criteria relied upon. Your treating provider may request a peer-to-peer discussion with a Cigna medical director. You have the right to appeal this decision in writing within 180 days of the date of this notice. Please refer to your plan materials for instructions on where to submit your appeal.

Sincerely,
Cigna Prior Authorization Review
clinical_context: The member is a 48-year-old woman with true resistant hypertension, with office blood pressures persisting around 168/104 mmHg and a mean 24-hour ambulatory pressure of 152/96 mmHg, despite maximally tolerated doses of three first-line agents from different classes - amlodipine 10 mg, chlorthalidone 25 mg, and losartan 100 mg - plus documented adherence on pharmacy refill data. A spironolactone trial was limited by hyperkalemia, and secondary causes were excluded. Her in-network cardiologist referred her for renal denervation because no in-network facility in her plan's network performs the procedure, and submitted a network gap-exception request with the prior authorization. Renal denervation received FDA approval in November 2023 for the treatment of hypertension and is supported in current adult AHA/ACC hypertension guidance for resistant hypertension, directly contradicting the experimental or investigational characterization. The geriatric hypertension management criteria the plan applied, which emphasize relaxed blood-pressure targets and deprescribing for frail elderly patients, do not apply to a 48-year-old adult, for whom guideline-based targets and treatment escalation are appropriate.

matrix_coverage.reasoning: matrix_cell matches field-for-field (Cigna, Prior Authorization, cardiology, 41-55, F, out_of_network_no_authorization); dx I10 is cardiology; renal denervation realistic; denial_rationale_seed foregrounds out_of_network_no_authorization (no gap exception on file); age 48 in band; not 71+ so null employer_archetype fine; experimental/geriatric prongs secondary.

scenario_realism.reasoning: Stacks three real Cigna tactics (OON-no-gap-exception, experimental/investigational label, misapplied geriatric guideline); clinical substrate sound (true resistant HTN by ABPM, three maxed agents, adherence, secondary causes excluded); renal denervation FDA-approved Nov 2023 and AHA/ACC-supported so the experimental label is a realistic stale-policy gap; frail-elderly targets on a 48-year-old is a believable, easily-rebutted overreach.

insurer_voice.reasoning: Correctly names Cigna and cites 'Cigna Medical Coverage Policy' with bureaucratic cadence and no wrong-insurer tells, but omits the signature 'Cigna Medical Necessity Guidelines'/'Milliman Care Guidelines (MCG)' fingerprint, leaving it generically-correct rather than unmistakably Cigna.
insurer_voice.improvement: Could add MCG/Medical Necessity Guidelines tell; accepted (passes, no wrong-insurer tells).

denial_logic.reasoning: Primary OON logic tracks the sub_tactic (gap exception not on file; vague 'in-network options available' deflection); realistically shoddy and not airtight, leaving appeal room. The stacked experimental + geriatric rationales function as realistic over-justification; no clean hallucinated named guideline; authentically exploitable.

clinical_realism.reasoning: Textbook triple therapy (amlodipine 10, chlorthalidone 25, losartan 100 = CCB + thiazide-like + ARB); correct resistant-HTN definition with ABPM confirmation (152/96); spironolactone fourth-line limited by hyperkalemia is physiologic; renal denervation FDA Nov 2023 and AHA/ACC support accurate; no invented drugs or impossible courses.

diagnosis_treatment_match.reasoning: Renal denervation is a legitimate device therapy studied (SPYRAL/RADIANCE) and FDA-approved (2023) for resistant hypertension; the dx-tx category match is exact.

diversity_delta.reasoning: Renal denervation for resistant HTN appears in no neighbour; closest cardiology procedures (TAVR, EP ablation, septal myectomy, RHC) are anatomically/indication unrelated; out_of_network_no_authorization denial reason is unique among neighbours. Distinct device, condition, and denial reason; not a near-duplicate.

diversity_statistical.reasoning: Max trigram-Jaccard: corpus=0.105 (nearest=case_185_aetna_mednec), neighbours=0.0. Thresholds: warn>=0.35, fail>=0.5.

safety_redactor.reasoning: Adult 48F hypertension procedure dispute; no SI/self-harm/child abuse/IPV/EOL/minor-gender care; commercial Cigna plan, geriatric criteria are an internal plan policy not Medicare/Medicaid framing. No banned topics.

contradiction_hunter.reasoning: Age (48F), dx (resistant HTN I10), and requested renal denervation agree across profile, letter, and context; date May 22, 2026 stable; the experimental and geriatric claims are the allowed appealable denial falsehoods the context rebuts; OON denial aligns with context (no in-network facility; gap exception submitted); no fact instability.

llm_tell_detector.reasoning: Formal insurance boilerplate and dense clinical recitation with specific values; no 'As an AI' tics, GPT vocabulary, listicles, or marketing filler; nothing at the >=0.7 conspicuous-tell bar.

overall_tone.reasoning: Cold procedural Cigna register, stacked vague rationales without committing to one basis (realistic), standard appeal boilerplate, no apology/marketing; clinical context plain factual with only analytic (not emotional) rebuttal framing.

financial_auditor.reasoning: No member out-of-pocket figure or dollar liability; OON/coverage language is benefit framing, not cost; only 'at no charge' for records access; silence on cost is standard; no Medicare/Medicaid conflation.

legal_auditor.reasoning: Cites a plausibly-named Cigna Medical Coverage Policy (no invented statute); concrete 180-day appeal window; omits external/IRO review and ERISA-specific disclosures (realistic strategic incompleteness, an attack vector not a defect); no Medicare framing on this commercial self-funded plan.

demographic_validator.reasoning: 48F with resistant hypertension is epidemiologically plausible and exactly the renal-denervation population; consistent female pronouns; no stereotyping. The geriatric-criteria misapplication is a policy flaw, not a demographic-validity defect.

scope_guard.reasoning: Cigna in-scope, Prior Authorization, self_funded employer-sponsored commercial (no Medicare/Medicaid/TRICARE/VA/marketplace), adult 48, member-facing written appeal/peer-to-peer only, no live-filing/attorney/court language.

date_sanity.reasoning: Request May 22, 2026; denial post-dates it; 180-day appeal window sensible; FDA Nov 2023 correctly precedes the request; coherent 2023->2026 stack; no impossible durations or vague hedges.

citation_traceability.reasoning: References 'Cigna Medical Coverage Policy' and 'geriatric hypertension management criteria' as un-named internal policies with no bulletin/edition/page and no fabricated alphanumeric; offers the specific criteria on request. Realistic frustrating vagueness without over-specification or hallucination.

appeal_difficulty.reasoning: Experimental label refuted by FDA Nov 2023 + AHA/ACC; geriatric criteria misapplied to a 48-year-old; OON basis undercut by submitted gap-exception request and no in-network facility performing the procedure; patient clearly meets resistant-HTN standard of care (three maxed agents, ABPM, spironolactone-limiting hyperkalemia, secondary causes excluded). Procedurally clean but substantive grounds collapse; easy appeal.
exploitable_weaknesses: Experimental/investigational label contradicted by FDA approval (Nov 2023) and AHA/ACC guidance for resistant hypertension
Geriatric hypertension criteria misapplied to a 48-year-old adult for whom guideline-based targets apply
Out-of-network denial undercut by submitted network gap-exception request and absence of any in-network facility performing the procedure
Patient clearly meets true resistant-hypertension standard of care (three maxed agents, documented adherence, spironolactone-limiting hyperkalemia, secondary causes excluded)
strong_defenses: Procedural disclosures are correctly stated: 180-day appeal window, peer-to-peer option, and free access to claim file and clinical criteria
